meta_title: HDB-Approved Dog Breeds Singapore: Full Rules | Curious Tails
meta_description: The complete HDB dog rules: one approved-breed dog per flat, the official 62-breed list explained, the $4,000 penalty, Project ADORE, and what to do if your dream breed isn't on it.
h1: The Complete HDB-Approved Dog Breeds List, Explained

entity_definitions.definition: HDB flats may keep one dog from HDB's approved list of 62 small breeds and crosses, such as the Bichon Frise, Toy Poodle, Maltese, Miniature Dachshund, and Chihuahua. Non-approved breeds like Corgis and Shiba Inus are not allowed in flats, and non-compliance risks a fine of up to $4,000.

faq.question: How many dogs can an HDB flat keep?
faq.answer_direction: One, from the approved list; fines up to $4,000 for non-compliance.

faq.question: Are Cavapoos and Maltipoos HDB-approved?
faq.answer_direction: Yes; crosses of approved small breeds are commonly accepted, and all five small crosses we carry qualify.

faq.question: Can I appeal for a Corgi or Golden Retriever?
faq.answer_direction: Not through any route we can promise; Project ADORE covers adopted local mixed breeds, not purchased non-approved breeds.

faq.question: What is Project ADORE?
faq.answer_direction: An AVS scheme letting HDB households adopt a medium-sized local mixed-breed dog under set conditions.

faq.question: Do condos follow the HDB list?
faq.answer_direction: No; private housing allows up to three dogs, subject to estate rules.

faq.question: Who handles the dog licence?
faq.answer_direction: We complete it with you at purchase; first-timers also do the free AVS Pet Ownership Course.
